title: astragalus australis var. cottonii
intro: Astragalus australis var. cottonii, also known as Cotton's milk vetch, is a plant species within the pea family (Fabaceae) that is native to Australia and New Zealand. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and immunomodulatory effects. The health benefits of Astragalus australis var. cottonii include its ability to boost the immune system, reduce stress, and improve respiratory function, making it a popular herbal remedy for conditions such as bronchitis and asthma. Astragalus australis var. cottonii can be prepared into various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and capsules, which are often used to promote overall well-being and support the body's natural healing processes.
